themes: model competition (GPT-5.5, Grok Build, Codex continuation)

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Simon Willison's quantified finding that Opus 4.7 uses 1.46x text tokens and up to 3x image tokens vs 4.6 at the same per-token price is the most concrete cost-impact analysis in the feed — directly relevant to anyone pricing AI product builds.

worthClicking.author: @simonw
worthClicking.why: Important follow-up clarifying that the 3x image cost only applies to high-resolution images; small images cost the same, which meaningfully changes the billing risk calculus.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Mollick's counterfactual — OpenAI could have suppressed reasoning model research by not publishing o1, forestalling DeepSeek — is a useful strategic frame for thinking about open vs. closed AI product positioning.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Grok Build and Grok Computer dropping together as a unified macOS/Windows desktop app is a meaningful distribution move worth tracking as a competitor to Codex and Claude Code.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: The piece on the real gap between casual Claude Code use and orchestrating it as a system — reportedly adopted at Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI internally — is directly relevant to AI product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Manifest's open-source model router that picks cheap vs. strong models per request in under 2ms with claimed 70% cost reduction is a concrete infra option for anyone building agent-heavy products.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: The argument that every human-designed paradigm — finance, communication, time management — must be rebuilt from first principles for agents is the sharpest strategic framing in the feed for agent-native product thinking.

worthClicking.author: @signulll
worthClicking.why: "If your roadmap is >30 days you're ngmi" is a pithy but high-signal provocation about AI-era product planning cycles that resonates with indie dev and fast-iteration strategy.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: A 30-minute workshop from the creator of Claude Code on vibe-coding is the most practical Claude Code resource in the feed for someone building or advising on AI-assisted dev workflows.

voices.why: Doing the actual empirical work on Opus 4.7 token cost behavior — the kind of ground-truth analysis product builders need before committing to a model.
Consistently the sharpest thinker on agent-native strategy and distribution dynamics in this feed; low noise-to-signal ratio.
Provides useful historical and strategic context on OpenAI's model release decisions that informs competitive positioning.

examples: aakashgupta multi-topic content farm (NASA, tennis longevity, Dragon Ball, Wolf of Wall Street)
RoundtableSpace crypto shilling (AAVE exploit, Polymarket trading bots)
fuckgrowth/aakashgupta newsletter subscription spam and engagement-bait polls
